tweets.text:
The permissioned path does not arrive as tyranny. It arrives as convenience. 

A society can lose its freedom this way without a single dramatic moment, simply by routing more of its thinking through infrastructure that answers to someone else.

We must protect open source and open source models

tweets.text:
Your controversial statement is suggesting that BTC is not "owned". 

It shows a misunderstanding of what ownership means.  It seems you've twisted yourself up into a semantic knot.  

If the purchaser of BTC doesn't own it why would they swap some other thing they own for it?  

If you care to discuss what it means to own something I am happy to engage but if you keep narrowly stating that BTC isnt owned by anyone I don't know where to go with that information that helps me or my readers.

tweets.text:
This is gonna upset a lot of people: 

But TA is astrology for traders.

It's confirmation bias + trading human psychology about entries. 

Kinda like how people frontran $SPCE from $SPCX IPO expecting retail to mess up tickers by trading psychology. 

$SIVE didn't go up 1900% because of the golden cross space comet firebreathing dragon candle that someone is trying to sell for $499. 

It's because markets are pricing in future revenue from $JBL, $GFS that got announced. 

$AXTI didn't go up 8000% because the golden waterfall candle alert sounded back at $8. 

it's because of InP substrate, game theory on ASP hikes, export controls, photonics demand, and others. 

If you want to figure out psychologically what other regards are believing, you use TA. 

But for determining the actual upside... nah

People have been drawing $120+ TAs on $IREN for the past idk how many months none of that crap matters when there's a $6B ATM that needs to be bought through first.

It's by theme (eg. $LITE to $AAOI relations), any news catalysts that affect forward revenue, projections, macro news, earnings, float dynamics, and so on. 

Then you can just derive what MC that company should be at. 

So for entry points, sure you can use TA. 

For determining where the stock heads, just throw the tyrannosaurs rex omega-green candle indicator out the window.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: @aleabitoreddit Do you use TA at all or are you 100% bottle neck research?

$SIVE didn't go up 1900% because of the golden cross space comet firebreathing dragon candle that someone is trying to sell for $499. 

It's because markets are pricing in future revenue from $JBL, $GFS that got announced. 

$AXTI didn't go up 8000% because the golden waterfall candle alert sounded back at $8. 

it's because of InP substrate, game theory on ASP hikes, export controls, photonics demand, and others. 

If you want to figure out psychologically what other regards are believing, you use TA. 

But for determining the actual upside... nah

People have been drawing $120+ TAs on $IREN for the past idk how many months none of that crap matters when there's a $6B ATM that needs to be bought through first.

It's by theme (eg. $LITE to $AAOI relations), any news catalysts that affect forward revenue, projections, macro news, earnings, float dynamics, and so on. 

Then you can just derive what MC that company should be at. 

So for entry points, sure you can use TA. 

For determining where the stock heads, just throw the tyrannosaurs rex omega-green candle indicator out the window.

tweets.quoted_tweet_text: BPS measures Bitcoin per common share before senior claims. CEBE BPS measures Bitcoin per common share after senior claims. CEBE is the conservative risk metric. BPS is the common equity growth metric. BTC Yield measures BPS execution.

thread_tweets.text:
2/2

At their peak last week, AI stocks were 49% of the S&P 500 (chart). 

This is the most concentrated the stock market has been on a single theme since the railroad stocks of the late 19th century. Higher when SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI are added, after they are all public.

Given this historic concentration, and AI and non-AI stocks are moving opposite each other (above), is the stock market effectively two asset classes

* AI
* Everything Else

tweets.text:
From "AI regulation is coming" to "AI regulation is here"

From Mexico border to global border

Fable/Mythos 5 ban was a wake up call for everybody
> China been promoting open intelligence so obviously they're getting more love
> Europe has been slacking and now they have to wake up and build their own intelligence
> Enterprises now have to think twice about sticking w/ 1 provider. Because if agents are integrated across internal workflows, what happen when the gov turn that off?
> Talents will prob have 3 choices — learn Mandarin, build sovereign AI for their country, or build open-source/decentralized intelligence

OpenRouter Fusion proves Fable 5 level of intelligence is achievable by aggregating open models together

Whatever happens, open & decentralized AI wins

Sharing my thoughts on this + relfections/takeaways from SuperAI in today's the After Hour EP.59

[Link in bio]
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Why this dump could be a great opportunity

> Rate hike already priced in
> Iran/Hormuz situation priced in
> The deal unlocks biggest catalyst for risk-on assets
> Oil down → gas down → inflation down → Fed can cut
> AI productivity = another catalyst
> More outputs with less https://t.co/dGtWJ9OWVF

tweets.text:
This week's Video:

The AI Mid-Cycle Slowdown: OpenAI, Anthropic, and the AI Price War

As Gavin Baker said on BG2 - “You've had a lot of stocks that forget climbing a mountain or hill. They've gone straight up a cliff.”

“They're tired. They need to rest.”

At the same time, the only people doubting AI are the perma-bears which means a two-sided consolidation is likely from here.

The easy part of the trade is over.

The next phase is about price compression at the model layer, rising costs at the physical layer but with continued rotation away from the spenders (hyperscalers) toward the companies receiving the capex.

This is not bubble collapse.

It is digestion.

Watch here:https://t.co/Z5JwstESCF
